step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of option B, 'Critical closing pressure.' This concept directly addresses the scenario described—pressure without any ongoing blood flow—and specifies precisely when circulation stops entirely by indicating full vessel occlusion. While other definitions like perfusion pressure relate to tissue oxygenation rather than absolute cessation of flow, they do not capture this specific moment of zero pressure causing lack of flow.
